result:
**Proposed goal: Add an Ocean Thermohaline Circulation & Global Current System simulation mode.**

This fills the most obvious gap in the project's Earth system coverage. You have Planetary Atmosphere & Weather, Plate Tectonics, and Earthquake/Seismic modes — but no ocean. The thermohaline "Great Ocean Conveyor Belt" is one of Earth's most critical climate systems and would pair naturally with the existing weather mode.

The mode would model:
- **Thermohaline circulation** — temperature and salinity fields driving density-gradient deep currents (cold salty water sinking at poles, warm surface return flow)
- **Wind-driven surface currents** — Ekman transport, western boundary intensification (Gulf Stream / Kuroshio), gyres
- **Deep water formation** — North Atlantic Deep Water (NADW) and Antarctic Bottom Water (AABW) convective sinking zones
- **Upwelling/downwelling** — coastal and equatorial upwelling bringing nutrients to surface, driving productivity
- **ENSO oscillation** — El Niño / La Niña Pacific thermocline seesaw with Walker circulation coupling
- **Freshwater hosing / circulation collapse** — ice sheet meltwater shutting down NADW formation (climate tipping point)
- 3 views (global current map with temperature/salinity overlay, ocean cross-section depth profile showing conveyor belt, 10-metric sparkline time series)
- 6 presets (Stable Modern Circulation, Gulf Stream Intensification, ENSO El Niño Event, Glacial Meltwater Hosing / AMOC Shutdown, Anoxic Ocean Stratification, Snowball Earth Frozen Ocean)
